module_signals.finding: Primary filings give high-confidence facts on Q2 FY25, full-cycle history, mix, and a dated price; normalized-earnings and HBM-margin durability are the medium-confidence gaps -> modest size haircut, not a cap to WATCH.

Memory up-cycle plus structural data-center/HBM mix shift (data center >50% of revenue, HBM >$1B/qtr); market overweighting a one-quarter GM wobble and underweighting the rising earnings floor. Context = cyclical_inflection with an exceptional_bottleneck HBM sleeve.

Durable sleeve = HBM + data-center DRAM (scarce, pricing power, qualified-in) vs commodity DRAM/NAND (no moat, SBU still loss-making in FY24). Rational 3-player DRAM oligopoly + leading-edge node; but HBM premium is a contestable bottleneck rent, not a permanent moat.

Clearly mid-cycle UP-leg (FY22 +$8.7B peak, FY23 -$5.8B trough, FY24 +$0.8B recovery, FY25 1H ~$6.9B annualized). ~15x run-rate but ~21-35x normalized through-cycle EPS; FCF only $857M vs $3.1B/qtr capex. Balance sheet survives (net debt ~$4.76B, ND/equity ~0.10). Confirms up-cycle + survivability but refutes the 'optically cheap' reflex -> net neutral.

Live risks: cyclical-peak trap (buying above mid-cycle earnings), oversupply/capex black hole, HBM-premium compression from Samsung/SK Hynix. No hard veto: profit pool is growing not eroding and balance sheet survives a normal downcycle -> warning that caps size, not a zero.

At $94.72 (post-~8% print de-rate) the entry is fair-not-cheap: ~15x up-leg run-rate, ~21-35x normalized. Acceptable for a small starter; real accumulation reserved $70-85; trim/no-chase >$115-120. Good company, only an acceptable buy-point.

binding_constraint: M5 cyclical-peak risk anchored by M4 normalization: paying ~21-35x normalized through-cycle earnings for a commodity-heavy memory cyclical in its up-leg caps the position to a small starter with adds staged lower; no hard veto because the balance sheet survives the cycle and the data-center/HBM profit pool is growing.
kill_criteria: Two consecutive quarters of declining data-center/HBM revenue AND gross margin (up-cycle rolls over before the structural floor is proven).
HBM ASP/premium visibly compressing as Samsung/SK Hynix capacity floods the accelerator socket.
Industry capex signalling 2026 oversupply with Micron inventory building faster than revenue (NRV-write-down regime returning).
Balance-sheet deterioration: net debt rising materially with durably negative through-cycle FCF.
runner_dissent: Mild. Harness logic (real bottleneck + survivable balance sheet + growing profit pool, no veto) supports a STARTER and I concur, but the entry is ABOVE mid-cycle earnings on a contestable HBM premium, so I deliberately kept initial size small (1.5%) and placed the true accumulation zone $10-25 lower. A strict cyclical-discipline investor could defend WATCH-until-$85; I chose a small STARTER because the data-center mix shift gives this cycle a credibly higher floor and the downside is survivable at this size. No post-as-of information was used.
